(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

> (1 comment)

The zfs_set_prop_str function returns a non-zero if the property wasn't set. zfs does not store property settings if they aren't supported. Setting those properties are best effort at file system creation time. If the property couldn't be set, then no harm is done. I also chose to use the zfs_set_prop_str approach because it wouldn't rely on any compile time definitions.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

It looks like this patch is introducing a problem in our testing environment due to recordsize=1024k causing all files on the OSTs to use 1MB blocks and consuming a lot of space.

 == sanity test 184c: Concurrent write and layout swap ==== 01:31:50 (1472002310)
 dd: failed to open '/mnt/lustre/d184c.sanity/184c/ref1': No space left on device
 dd: failed to open '/mnt/lustre/d184c.sanity/184c/ref2': No space left on device

It looks like we need to rebase this patch onto http://review.whamcloud.com/18441 or wait until it lands so that it will pass testing.  Until now this patch has always failed sanity in review-zfs-part-1.

commit.subject: LU-8342 utils: Set dnodesize/recordsize at zfs dataset create
commit.message:
LU-8342 utils: Set dnodesize/recordsize at zfs dataset create

After the zfs dataset is created, attempt to set the
dnodesize and recordsize properties. Moved xattr=sa to be
consistent with the new method of setting dataset properties.
